Patch Set 1:

Ellis, this was submitted as a new patch (new Change-Id), and the previous version of this patch https://review.whamcloud.com/54766 was abandoned.

Please push new versions of a patch using the *same* Change-Id as the original version.  Otherwise, it is difficult to compare the old and new versions of the patch (no inter-diff possible) and it hides the review comments on the previous version.

You should have the Git hooks installed from inside the Lustre tree:

 $ cd .git/hooks
 $ ln -sf ../../contrib/git-hooks/*commit-msg .

and it will add the Change-Id: line to each commit message *before* it is submitted, so that it is kept over subsequent pushes.  If you do something to the patch locally that loses the commit message for whatever reason then you could always just copy-paste the Change-Id: line from the patch in Gerrit.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Mark -1 until testing is done and I update the commit message.  Ping me if I forget.

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Mark -1 until testing is done and I update the commit message.  Ping me if I forget.

Thanks for making this change.  This was a mechanical oversight again when going from our environment to yours.  Our bug reads "Upstream: LTest: Sanityn104 suite - test_73 failed," and as part of revising to meet the git commit title rules you guys use I wrongly converted this to sanityn 104.  We divide our testing into batches to parallelize things internally, so this was just the sanityn suite that runs up through test 104, not that test 104 was failing.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 37fa5a5c0cb154630f4e244c8b2919bd908470f1 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-17362 build: Update ZFS version to 2.1.15

commit.subject: LU-17736 tests: Fix sanityn/73 for test machines with auditd
commit.message:
LU-17736 tests: Fix sanityn/73 for test machines with auditd

getfattr performs one stat followed by two getxattr syscalls against
the provided file.  Normally, the stat results in no getxattr calls
internally (as it's not something stat is required to return).

However, if auditd is enabled AND one of the rules includes a
filesystem-specific rule such as watch directory X and record if it's
modified, then for every lookup (each of the three syscalls includes
one) an additional getxattr will be performed, resulting in 5 total
getxattrs.

Because there is significant fuzz here, revise the check to be
at minimum the two "expected" getxattrs but allow for more.
Comments have been added explaining this.

Signed-off-by: Ellis Wilson <elliswilson@microsoft.com>
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanityn env=ONLY=73,ONLY_REPEAT=10
Change-Id: I0da5c2a5331f7dba4e65051a073e2bec05327a25
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54809
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
